items.title: L'Oreal to Take Control of Gucci Beauty From July 2027, a Year Early
items.summary: Kering, L'Oreal and Coty agreed to accelerate the handover of the Gucci Beauty license: Coty's existing deal (due to expire mid-2028) will be redeemed early, with L'Oreal covering roughly 70% of termination costs and Coty receiving about $400 million plus inventory support, under a new 50-year exclusive L'Oreal-Gucci partnership starting July 2027. Kering's CEO framed it as accelerating 'the next chapter of Gucci Beauty a year earlier than planned'; L'Oreal called Gucci 'a significant additional growth engine.'

items.title: Law Firm Investigates Kering Over Tariff Price Hikes Never Refunded to Consumers
items.summary: CRITICAL ANGLE: a US plaintiffs' firm announced it is investigating whether Kering raised US retail prices on Gucci, Saint Laurent, Balenciaga and other house products to pass along 2025 tariffs, then kept the higher prices after the Supreme Court ruled the tariffs unlawful in February 2026 -- while also potentially recovering the same tariff costs from the government. No lawsuit has been filed and no wrongdoing has been determined; the notice solicits US consumers who bought Kering-brand goods during the tariff period.

items.title: Karl-Anthony Towns Wears Gucci Tuxedo With Tiffany Brooch at 2026 ESPYs
items.summary: NBA player Karl-Anthony Towns, fresh off the Knicks' playoff run, wore a light wool Gucci suit with black satin details at the 2026 ESPYs in New York, paired with an archival Tiffany brooch; part of wider Black-celebrity-couples red carpet coverage. Standard celebrity-dressing placement coverage rather than organic commentary.

items.title: Kering Stock Edges Higher as Gucci Recovery and Margin Focus Shape Investor Debate
items.summary: Financial-desk piece noting Kering shares trading in a narrow range as investors weigh early signs of a Gucci turnaround against still-depressed profitability: FY2025 group revenue near EUR19.6bn (down from over EUR20bn at peak), operating margin near 24% (vs over 25% at peak), and Gucci revenue around EUR10bn versus an EUR11bn prior peak, with Saint Laurent and Bottega Veneta partly offsetting Gucci's slower momentum. Frames Gucci's sustained recovery as the key open question for the stock.

items.title: PurseForum Gucci Subforum: Ongoing Deals-Chat and Flora Threads Active
items.summary: Community forum activity remains centered on practical buyer topics -- an active 'Gucci sales-outlet-web-and-auction deals' chat thread and the running '2026 Gucci Flora' thread comparing Demna-era Flora-print bags to archival prints -- rather than reaction to any single news event; tone is transactional/collector-focused (styles, hardware, authenticity) rather than strongly positive or negative.
